steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Main Idea question. The decisive clue is the word because in the sentence “The researchers have concluded that these settlements were culturally linked to Izapa because each of the settlements is the same age and configured in the same manner as Izapa, with a pyramid to the north and a plaza to the south.” The main idea must capture both the conclusion (cultural link) and its basis (matching construction).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Researchers have determined that the arrangement of Izapa's structures was based on those of other nearby settlements.: Reverses the direction. The text never says Izapa copied the smaller settlements; the shared layout is the evidence for a link, not a source-of-influence claim. ✗
  • Choice B — Cultural ceremonies in Izapa seem to have played a more important role for its residents than those in smaller, surrounding settlements did.: Unsupported comparison. The text says residents likely performed similar ceremonies, never that ceremonies mattered more in one place than another. ✗
  • Choice C — Although archaeologists have learned much about Izapa over years of research, they have only recently found the smaller settlements that surrounded it.: Misidentifies the main idea. The recency of the discovery is background context, not the central point; the text never says how long archaeologists have studied Izapa. ✗
  • Choice D — Researchers have inferred that Izapa was related to the smaller settlements that surrounded it based in part on the similarity of their construction.: Matches the passage exactly. It names the inference (cultural link/relationship) and the evidence (similar construction). ✓
That matches Choice D — Researchers have inferred that Izapa was related to the smaller settlements that surrounded it based in part on the similarity of their construction..
